summary: Largest Incahuasi khipu by cord count — 490 cords, 2+-ARBRE depth L5, 9 clusters. Only 1 STRING (qaqa). NB(232)/AB(181) dominant. 115 EMPTY (23%). 10 L-1. A major commodity or production register.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  490 cords (largest at Incahuasi). 1 STRING (qaqa), 364 INT, 115 EMPTY (23%), 10 L-1.
  Architecture: 2+-ARBRE, depth L5. 9 clusters. Colors: NB(232), AB(181), KB(28), MB(27), GG(8).
  Provenance: Incahuasi.

NARRATIVE
  Four hundred and ninety cords — the largest khipu excavated at Incahuasi, deeper than any other at five levels of hierarchy. Nine clusters divide the register. One word: *qaqa*, the universal label. The rest is numbers, organized in a tree of dark and light thread, counting something at a scale that required the largest register the garrison kept.
